entry.title: spicy hot take but anyone with enough money to be paying inheritance tax should absolutely be paying inheritance tax. it...
entry.summary: spicy hot take but anyone with enough money to be paying inheritance tax should absolutely be paying inheritance tax. it does not apply to a majority of Brits

entry.title: Breaking news: man who could end world hunger and make serious roads into contesting climate change but instead chooses...
entry.summary: Breaking news: man who could end world hunger and make serious roads into contesting climate change but instead chooses to make porn-AI accessible and cheerlead for fascists can’t work out why he’s not happy. 🧐

entry.title: Man arrested for murder after student's death
entry.summary: The body of 19-year-old Carla Georgescu was found in student accommodation on Thursday, police say.

entry.title: Weekly Crime Digest: 1119 offences across Lancashire councils
entry.summary: Latest police data shows 1119 crimes reported across monitored Lancashire boroughs. Burnley: 1119 crimes (364 violent crime). Full ward-by-ward breakdowns available via AI DOGE.

entry.title: Politics Roundup: 16 key stories
entry.summary: Latest politics news from across Lancashire.

entry.title: Blackburn Labour canvassers urge voters to support ‘unity’ not ‘division’
entry.summary: Labour supporters were in Gorton and Denton constituency over the weekend ahead of the by-election at the end of the month.

entry.title: Drink driver arrested after breakdown help call
entry.summary: The 39-year-old man was found "ranting and raving" after breaking down and raising the alarm himself.

entry.title: So as I struggled with miscarriage after miscarriage before having my 🌈 baby, Reform would seriously think I should pay...
entry.summary:
So as I struggled with miscarriage after miscarriage before having my 🌈 baby, Reform would seriously think I should pay more tax during that agony.
This policy isn’t just a slippery slope to a handmaids tale dystopian future - it’s a deliberate step towards it www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/poli...

entry.title: Trial date set for man accused of drug possession with intent to supply in Burnley
entry.summary: A Burnley man accused of possessing drugs with intent to supply will stand trial later this year after pleading not guilty.

entry.title: Woman was supplying drugs to neighbours to fund her own habit
entry.summary: A Nelson woman who admitted supplying cannabis to neighbours to fund her own drug habit has been given a community order.

entry.title: ‘A moment we feared would never come’ - families celebrate lifeline for care homes
entry.summary: The niece of a woman who lives in one of the Lancashire County Council-run care homes whose futures had been in doubt says confirmation that none of them will be closed marks a “victory” in the community campaigns to protect the facilities.

entry.title: Residents baffled by bus station shake-up — but councillors say fears are unfounded
entry.summary: Colne residents and businesses are confused about a bus station revamp and traffic flow changes – but local councillors insist it will improve things for everyone.

entry.title: New Clitheroe pump track planned as £100,000 funding set aside
entry.summary: Clitheroe could get a new pump track after Ribble Valley Borough Council earmarked up to £100,000 from the UK Shared Prosperity Fund for the project

entry.title: Burnley residents urged to reject Blackburn merger in devolution consultation
entry.summary: The leader of Burnley Council has urged borough residents to reject a merger with Blackburn in a local government reorganisation consultation.

entry.title: Burnley man jailed after threatening to kill child after sexually assaulting her
entry.summary: A Burnley man who threatened to kill a teenager if she did not let him sexually assault her has been jailed

entry.title: In response to my post about the launch of the 'National Cancer Plan' on substack, someone responsed with: "Great. But...
entry.summary:
In response to my post about the launch of the 'National Cancer Plan' on substack, someone responsed with:
"Great. But how about ending homelessness, taxing the rich nationalising water and ending the cost of living crisis?"
So I responsed with this...
1/9

entry.title: 2 week long temporary traffic lights and other major roadworks starting across Wyre this week
entry.summary: This week, numerous roadworks are beginning across Wyre, including two week long road closures.

entry.title: Four lane closures and other major roadworks starting across Preston next week
entry.summary: Next week, numerous roadworks are beginning across Preston, including four lane closures on major roads.

entry.title: Drone photos reveal scale of huge fire
entry.summary: Photos released by the fire service show the gutted shell of an indoor play area destroyed in the blaze.

entry.title: Have your say on Lancashire’s new-look local councils - here are the five options as consultation launches
entry.summary: Have your say on Lancashire’s new-look local councils - here are the five options as consultation launches Lancashire Evening Post

entry.title: Plans for private gym and golf simulator extension to rural house approved by council
entry.summary: Plans to extend a rural home near Alston to create a private gym and golf simulator room have been approved by the council

entry.title: Great Harwood's Mercer Hall wins £60k grant from Arts Council
entry.summary: Great Harwood’s historic Mercer Hall has been given a £60,000 grant to develop a new arts and culture programme

entry.title: Student, 19, found dead in University of Lancashire campus halls of residence as police make arrest
entry.summary: Police are investigating the suspicious death of a 19-year-old, found in Ribble Hall on the University of Lancashire campus.

entry.title: St Annes woman, 30, arrested after what police found inside car
entry.summary: A 30-year-old woman from St Annes has been arrested.

entry.title: Call 999 if you see this wanted man with links to Lancaster, Morecambe and Heysham
entry.summary: Leigh McGinley, 35, is wanted for assaulting police. He is linked to Lancaster, Morecambe and Heysham.

entry.title: Future Olympics could be held in the North of England as leaders say 'we are ready'
entry.summary: The North of England could host the Olympics for the first time, as mayors and leaders propose a UK bid anchored in the region, aiming to accelerate regeneration and unity.

entry.title: Man sentenced to prison for downloading indecent images of children
entry.summary: A Haslingden man has been sentenced to more than three years in prison for downloading indecent images of children.

entry.title: Eco-campaigners clash over moorland wind farm that could power 100,000 homes
entry.summary: Campaigners have hit out at green activists for backing the wind farm scheme planned for Scout Moor

entry.title: Missing schoolgirl remembered as crowds turn out to Ramadan Souk in Blackburn
entry.summary: Organisers wanted to replicate the souk events of North Africa, where people come together ahead of the Muslim holy month of Ramadan.

entry.title: Here's how many HMOs there are in Preston as tougher rules planned
entry.summary: Preston residents and landlords are being invited to have their say on plans to tighten up local rules surrounding the conversion of properties into houses of multiple occupation (HMOs).

entry.title: 'My body and mind were screaming at me to stop' - Former CEO shares difficult story with Blackpool
entry.summary: John Ainsworth, former divisional CEO, reveals the impact of executive pressure on his mental health and his journey to recovery.

entry.title: Accrington Stanley fans given the chance to speak to management directy - fans forum announced
entry.summary: Accrington Stanley will host a Fans’ Forum at the Wham Stadium next week - giving supporters the chance to engage directly with the club’s leadership.

entry.title: Teenage urban explorers in quarry mud rescue
entry.summary: The five teenagers became stuck in mud while "urban exploring" at the site, police said.

entry.title: King Charles to visit Clitheroe in Ribble Valley
entry.summary: The King is expected to meet local manufacturers and members of the public when he visits Clitheroe.

entry.title: 'We brought Chinese New Year party to Lancaster'
entry.summary: Meet the young graduates who put Lancaster on the map for spectacular Chinese New Year celebrations.
